instruction:
Implement file upload and inline comments systems for Ship.

For file uploads, create `routes/files.ts` with auth middleware: POST /api/files/upload-request (validates filename/mimeType/sizeBytes with Zod, blocks dangerous extensions like .exe/.bat/.sh/.dll/.jar/.ps1, creates a files row with status=pending, returns a presigned S3 upload URL if S3_UPLOADS_BUCKET is configured or a local upload endpoint for dev), POST /api/files/upload/:id (for local dev: accepts multipart form data, writes to uploads/ directory, updates files row to status=uploaded), GET /api/files/:id (returns file metadata + download URL). For S3: use @aws-sdk/client-s3 and @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner with 15-minute URL expiration. For local dev: serve uploads/ as static files. Set max file size to 1GB. Validate UUID format on all ID params to prevent path traversal.

For comments, create `routes/comments.ts` with: GET /api/documents/:id/comments (list all comment threads for a document, grouped by comment_id), POST /api/documents/:id/comments (create a new comment thread with comment_id matching the TipTap comment mark, or reply to existing via parent_id), PATCH /api/comments/:id (update comment content), POST /api/comments/:id/resolve (set resolved_at timestamp), DELETE /api/comments/:id (only author or admin can delete). Create `routes/backlinks.ts` with: GET /api/documents/:id/backlinks (query document_links where target_id matches, return source documents with title and type), POST /api/documents/:id/links (upsert document_links from extracted mentions/links in content).

On the frontend, create `src/services/upload.ts` with: requestUpload (calls upload-request endpoint), uploadFile (either uploads directly to presigned S3 URL or to the local endpoint), trackUploadProgress. Create `src/services/uploadTracker.ts` to track active uploads and warn on navigation (UploadNavigationWarning component). Create `src/hooks/useCommentsQuery.ts` for fetching and mutating comments via TanStack Query.

Verify: upload an image via the API, confirm it returns a valid URL, embed it in a TipTap document via the editor, confirm it renders. Create a comment thread on a document, reply to it, resolve it, confirm the resolved_at timestamp is set.

steps.description: Rework the backend comments route to match the requested contract and eliminate likely verification/type/runtime errors from the prior partial implementation. Add explicit UUID validation for document and comment params, return GET /api/documents/:id/comments grouped by comment_id thread instead of a flat array, add POST /api/comments/:id/resolve with resolved_at timestamp handling, and fix DELETE /api/comments/:id authorization so author or workspace admin can delete while PATCH content edits remain author-only. Keep app wiring consistent with the split document/comment routers.

Repair the missing/incorrect backend test coverage for the comments contract. Create or update dedicated route tests to cover grouped thread responses, replies via parent_id, resolve endpoint behavior, UUID validation failures, and admin-authorized deletion so the watchdog errors from the previous run are addressed by concrete assertions.

Fix the frontend comments query layer to match the corrected backend response shape and operations. Update the exported types so consumers receive grouped comment threads rather than a flat list, add a dedicated resolve mutation that hits POST /api/comments/:id/resolve instead of overloading PATCH, and align create/update/delete invalidation behavior with the actual document-scoped API used by the editor.

Update all current frontend comment consumers to use the revised grouped-thread types and resolve flow so the editor/comment rendering code remains type-safe and functional after the API correction. This includes the TipTap comment display extension and the editor integration points that currently assume a flat comment array and PATCH-based resolve behavior.

Bring the upload service into strict alignment with the backend files API to resolve the remaining upload verification issues. Replace the stale assumptions about raw-body local uploads and S3 confirm responses with the actual contract: upload-request should return method/uploadType/headers consistently, local uploads should send multipart form-data to POST /api/files/upload/:id, and metadata/download URL handling should use the backend's returned fields without relying on nonexistent properties.

Propagate the corrected upload contract through all upload consumers so no stale assumptions remain. Update the upload tracker-facing editor entry points and navigation warning plumbing to work with the revised upload progress/results, and make the warning UI reflect the tracker/context behavior that actually exists after the service fixes.

     5	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;

     7	-- Workspaces
     8	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
     9	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    10	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    11	  sprint_start_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    12	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    13	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    14	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    15	);
    16	
    17	-- Users and auth (global identity - users can belong to multiple workspaces)
    18	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    19	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    20	  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    21	  password_hash TEXT,  -- NULL if using PIV-only auth
    22	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    23	  is_super_admin BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    24	  last_workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    25	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,           -- PIV certificate X.509 Subject DN
    26	  piv_first_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ, -- When user first logged in via PIV
    27	  last_auth_provider VARCHAR(50), -- 'fpki_validator', 'caia', null (legacy)
    28	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    29	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    30	);
    31	
    32	-- Case-insensitive email uniqueness (prevents duplicate users with different casing)
    33	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS users_email_lower_unique ON users (LOWER(email));
    34	
    35	-- Workspace memberships (users can be in multiple workspaces with different roles)
    36	-- AUTHORIZATION ONLY: This table controls access. Person documents (content layer) are separate.
    37	-- Person docs link to users via properties.user_id, NOT via this table.
    38	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_memberships (
    39	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    40	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    41	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    42	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    43	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    44	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    45	  UNIQUE(workspace_id, user_id)
    46	);
    47	
    48	-- Workspace invites (email invite flow)
    49	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_invites (
    50	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    51	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    52	  email TEXT NOT NULL,
    53	  token TEXT UNIQUE,  -- NULL for PIV invites (certificate proves identity)
    54	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    55	  invited_by_user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    56	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,  -- X.509 Subject DN for PIV invites
    57	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    58	  used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    59	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    60	);
    61	
    62	-- Audit logs (compliance-grade logging)
    63	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (
    64	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    65	  workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for global actions
    66	  actor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for failed login attempts
    67	  impersonating_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- If super-admin is impersonating
    68	  action TEXT NOT NULL,
    69	  resource_type TEXT,
    70	  resource_id UUID,
    71	  details JSONB,
    72	  ip_address INET,
    73	  user_agent TEXT,
    74	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    75	);
    76	
    77	-- Sessions with 15-minute inactivity timeout and 12-hour absolute timeout
    78	-- Session ID is TEXT (hex string from crypto.randomBytes) not UUID for enhanced security
    79	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
    80	  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    81	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    82	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    83	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    84	  last_activity TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    85	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    86	  -- Session binding data for audit and security
    87	  user_agent TEXT,
    88	  ip_address TEXT
    89	);
    90	
    91	-- OAuth state (survives server restarts during auth flow)
    92	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS oauth_state (
    93	  state_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    94	  nonce TEXT NOT NULL,
    95	  code_verifier TEXT NOT NULL,
    96	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    97	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL
    98	);
    99	
   100	-- Document types enum
   101	DO $$ BEGIN
   102	  CREATE TYPE document_type AS ENUM ('wiki', 'issue', 'program', 'project', 'sprint', 'person', 'weekly_plan', 'weekly_retro', 'standup', 'weekly_review');
   103	EXCEPTION
   104	  WHEN duplicate_object THEN null;
   105	END $$;
   106	
   107	-- Core document table (unified model - EVERYTHING IS A DOCUMENT)
   108	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS documents (
   109	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   110	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   111	  document_type document_type NOT NULL DEFAULT 'wiki',
   112	  title TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Untitled',
   113	
   114	  -- TipTap JSON content stored as JSONB (shared by ALL document types)
   115	  content JSONB DEFAULT '{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph"}]}',
   116	
   117	  -- Yjs binary state for collaboration (shared by ALL document types)
   118	  yjs_state BYTEA,
   119	
   120	  -- Hierarchy (cascade delete: deleting parent deletes all children)
   121	  parent_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   122	  position INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   123	
   124	  -- Associations: program, project, and sprint relationships are stored in document_associations table
   125	  -- Legacy columns (project_id, sprint_id, program_id) were removed by migrations 027 and 029.
   126	  -- Use document_associations table for all relationship queries.
   127	
   128	  -- Type-specific properties stored as JSONB
   129	  -- Issue properties: state, priority, assignee_id, source, rejection_reason
   130	  -- Program/Project properties: color
   131	  -- Sprint properties: start_date, end_date, sprint_status, plan
   132	  -- Person properties: user_id (links to users.id), email, capacity_hours, skills
   133	  properties JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
   134	
   135	  -- Keep these as columns for indexing/relationships/sequences
   136	  ticket_number INTEGER,  -- Auto-increment per workspace, needed for display_id
   137	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- For filtering archived items
   138	  deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- For trash/soft delete (30 day retention)
   139	
   140	  -- Status timestamps (for issues)
   141	  started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,    -- When issue status first changed to in_progress
   142	  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status first changed to done
   143	  cancelled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status changed to cancelled
   144	  reopened_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- When issue was reopened after being done/cancelled
   145	
   146	  -- Document conversion tracking
   147	  converted_to_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,   -- Points to new doc (on archived original)
   148	  converted_from_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL, -- Points to original doc (on new doc)
   149	  converted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   150	  converted_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   151	  original_type VARCHAR(50),    -- Original document_type when first created
   152	  conversion_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of times converted
   153	
   154	  -- Timestamps
   155	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   156	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   157	  created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   158	
   159	  -- Document visibility (private = creator only, workspace = all members)
   160	  visibility TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'workspace' CHECK (visibility IN ('private', 'workspace')),
   161	
   162	  -- Prevent self-referencing parent
   163	  CONSTRAINT documents_no_self_parent CHECK (id != parent_id)
   164	);
   165	
   166	-- Function and trigger to prevent circular parent references
   167	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_circular_parent()
   168	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   169	DECLARE
   170	  current_parent UUID;
   171	  depth INT := 0;
   172	  max_depth INT := 100;
   173	BEGIN
   174	  IF NEW.parent_id IS NULL THEN
   175	    RETURN NEW;
   176	  END IF;
   177	  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.parent_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NEW.parent_id THEN
   178	    RETURN NEW;
   179	  END IF;
   180	  current_parent := NEW.parent_id;
   181	  WHILE current_parent IS NOT NULL AND depth < max_depth LOOP
   182	    IF current_parent = NEW.id THEN
   183	      RAISE EXCEPTION 'Circular reference detected: document % cannot be a descendant of itself', NEW.id;
   184	    END IF;
   185	    SELECT parent_id INTO current_parent FROM documents WHERE id = current_parent;
   186	    depth := depth + 1;
   187	  END LOOP;
   188	  IF depth >= max_depth THEN
   189	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Maximum nesting depth (%) exceeded while checking for circular reference', max_depth;
   190	  END IF;
   191	  RETURN NEW;
   192	END;
   193	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   194	
   195	DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS prevent_circular_parent_trigger ON documents;
   196	CREATE TRIGGER prevent_circular_parent_trig

     2	 * Comment schemas - Inline document comments with threading and resolve

     4	
     5	import { z, registry } from '../registry.js';
     6	import { UuidSchema, DateTimeSchema, UserReferenceSchema } from './common.js';
     7	
     8	// ============== Comment Response ==============
     9	
    10	export const CommentResponseSchema = z.object({
    11	  id: UuidSchema,
    12	  document_id: UuidSchema,
    13	  comment_id: UuidSchema.openapi({ description: 'Thread identifier matching TipTap mark commentId' }),
    14	  parent_id: UuidSchema.nullable().openapi({ description: 'Parent comment ID for replies, null for root comments' }),
    15	  content: z.string(),
    16	  resolved_at: DateTimeSchema.nullable().openapi({ description: 'When the thread was resolved, null if unresolved' }),
    17	  author: UserReferenceSchema,
    18	  created_at: DateTimeSchema,
    19	  updated_at: DateTimeSchema,
    20	}).openapi('CommentResponse');
    21	
    22	registry.register('CommentResponse', CommentResponseSchema);
    23	
    24	// ============== Create Comment ==============
    25	
    26	export const CreateCommentSchema = z.object({
    27	  comment_id: UuidSchema.openapi({ description: 'Thread identifier (generated client-side, matches TipTap mark)' }),
    28	  content: z.string().min(1).max(10000),
    29	  parent_id: UuidSchema.optional().openapi({ description: 'Parent comment ID to reply to' }),
    30	}).openapi('CreateComment');
    31	
    32	registry.register('CreateComment', CreateCommentSchema);
    33	
    34	// ============== Update Comment ==============
    35	
    36	export const UpdateCommentSchema = z.object({
    37	  content: z.string().min(1).max(10000).optional(),
    38	  resolved_at: z.union([DateTimeSchema, z.null()]).optional().openapi({
    39	    description: 'Set to ISO datetime to resolve, null to un-resolve',
    40	  }),
    41	}).openapi('UpdateComment');
    42	
    43	registry.register('UpdateComment', UpdateCommentSchema);
    44	
    45	// ============== Register Paths ==============
    46	
    47	registry.registerPath({
    48	  method: 'get',
    49	  path: '/api/documents/{id}/comments',
    50	  operationId: 'list_document_comments',
    51	  summary: 'List comments for a document',
    52	  tags: ['Comments'],
    53	  request: {
    54	    params: z.object({ id: UuidSchema }),
    55	  },
    56	  responses: {
    57	    200: {
    58	      description: 'List of comments grouped by thread',
    59	      content: { 'application/json': { schema: z.array(CommentResponseSchema) } },
    60	    },
    61	  },
    62	});
    63	
    64	registry.registerPath({
    65	  method: 'post',
    66	  path: '/api/documents/{id}/comments',
    67	  operationId: 'create_document_comment',
    68	  summary: 'Create a comment on a document',
    69	  tags: ['Comments'],
    70	  request: {
    71	    params: z.object({ id: UuidSchema }),
    72	    body: { content: { 'application/json': { schema: CreateCommentSchema } } },
    73	  },
    74	  responses: {
    75	    201: {
    76	      description: 'Created comment',
    77	      content: { 'application/json': { schema: CommentResponseSchema } },
    78	    },
    79	  },
    80	});
    81	
    82	registry.registerPath({
    83	  method: 'patch',
    84	  path: '/api/comments/{id}',
    85	  operationId: 'update_comment',
    86	  summary: 'Update a comment (edit content or resolve/un-resolve)',
    87	  tags: ['Comments'],
    88	  request: {
    89	    params: z.object({ id: UuidSchema }),
    90	    body: { content: { 'application/json': { schema: UpdateCommentSchema } } },
    91	  },
    92	  responses: {
    93	    200: {
    94	      description: 'Updated comment',
    95	      content: { 'application/json': { schema: CommentResponseSchema } },
    96	    },
    97	  },
    98	});
    99	
   100	registry.registerPath({
   101	  method: 'delete',

   103	  operationId: 'delete_comment',
   104	  summary: 'Delete a comment',
   105	  tags: ['Comments'],

   108	  },

   114	  },
   115	});

Set up the PostgreSQL database layer for Ship. Create a `db/` directory under the API package with: `client.ts` (pg Pool with connection pooling, idle timeout, statement timeout, graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT), `schema.sql` (the full schema), and a `migrations/` folder for incremental changes.

Important path constraint: the canonical deliverable must live under `api/db/`, not only under the legacy `api/src/db/` path that may already exist in the repo. If existing runtime imports still point at `api/src/db/*`, convert those files into thin wrappers or re-exports only after the real implementation exists under `api/db/*`. When converting a legacy file into a wrapper, replace the entire file contents so only the wrapper/re-export remains; do not prepend exports onto the old implementation body. Do not treat editing only `api/src/db/*` as completion.

The schema must implement Ship's "everything is a document" model. Core tables: `workspaces`, `users` (with email, password_hash, x509_subject_dn, is_super_admin), `workspace_memberships` (role: admin|member), `sessions` (TEXT primary key from crypto.randomBytes, with last_activity and ip_address), `documents` (unified table with document_type ENUM for wiki/issue/program/project/sprint/person/weekly_plan/weekly_retro/standup/weekly_review, JSONB properties, JSONB content for TipTap, BYTEA yjs_state, parent_id self-reference with circular-reference prevention trigger, visibility private|workspace, soft-delete via deleted_at, ticket_number auto-increment per workspace), `document_associations` (junction table with relationship_type ENUM: parent/project/sprint/program), `document_history`, `document_snapshots`, `audit_logs`, `comments` (threaded with parent_id), `files`, `document_links` (for backlinks), `api_tokens`, `workspace_invites`, `oauth_state`, `sprint_iterations`, and `issue_iterations`. Add pg_trgm extension and comprehensive indexes (GIN on properties, trigram on titles, partial indexes on archived_at/deleted_at).

Write a `seed.ts` that creates a demo workspace, admin user (password hashed with bcryptjs), and sample documents. Write a `migrate.ts` that applies SQL files from `migrations/` in order. The initial canonical migration file must be `api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql`. Verify by running `pnpm db:migrate` and `pnpm db:seed` against a local PostgreSQL instance, then querying each table to confirm it exists with correct columns. Before declaring completion, confirm that `api/db/client.ts`, `api/db/migrate.ts`, `api/db/seed.ts`, `api/db/schema.sql`, and `api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql` all exist.

Instruction: Implement the TipTap rich-text editor component with collaborative editing for Ship documents. This is the core editing experience used across all document types (wiki, issue, project, program, weekly plans, etc.).

Create `src/components/Editor.tsx` (or `UnifiedEditor.tsx`) that initializes a TipTap editor with StarterKit, plus these extensions: Collaboration (Yjs binding to a shared Y.Doc fragment named 'default'), CollaborationCursor (awareness with user name/color), Placeholder, Link, TaskList+TaskItem, Table+TableRow+TableCell+TableHeader, CodeBlockLowlight, Dropcursor. Connect to the WebSocket collaboration server at `ws://localhost:3000/collaboration/{docType}:{docId}` using y-websocket's WebsocketProvider. Use y-indexeddb for offline persistence (IndexedDB provider). Handle the cache-clear message (type 3) from the server by clearing IndexedDB before sync.

Create custom extensions: MentionExtension (trigger with @, fetch suggestions from /api/search/mentions, render as inline chip linking to the mentioned document/person), ImageUpload (drag-and-drop/paste images, upload to /api/files, insert as resizable image node), FileAttachment (upload non-image files, render as download link), SlashCommands (trigger with /, show a menu of block types: heading, bullet list, numbered list, task list, code block, table, image, divider), HypothesisBlock (custom node for embedding plan/hypothesis content that gets extracted server-side).

Create `src/components/editor/BacklinksPanel.tsx` that fetches backlinks for the current document from /api/documents/:id/backlinks and displays them as a list below the editor. Create `src/components/editor/CommentDisplay.tsx` for inline comments (highlight marked text, show comment thread in a popover, support replies and resolve).

Create a properties sidebar that renders next to the editor via React Portal into `#properties-portal`. The sidebar shows document metadata: type, status (for issues), priority, assignee (PersonCombobox), belongs_to associations (program/project/sprint chips), visibility toggle, created/updated timestamps. Changes in the sidebar call PATCH on the document API and update via TanStack Query mutation.

Wire the editor into the document page: when navigating to /documents/:id, fetch the document, initialize the Yjs provider, render the editor with the properties sidebar. Handle WebSocket close codes: 4100 (document converted, redirect to new doc), 4101 (content updated via API, reconnect), 4403 (access revoked, show error).

Verify: open a document, type text, confirm it persists (reload and content appears), open the same document in two tabs, type in one, confirm text appears in the other within 1 second, confirm cursor presence shows the other user's name and color.
